# Questions in Livestream 80

Live Stream #80: Drs Falk and Kennedy Debate After Party

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=676" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:16</a> | Did Titus Kennedy knowingly misstate things, or was he misled by confirmation bias? | Falk says some points, such as the handling of strata and destruction layers, are basic enough that Kennedy should have known they were wrong. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=888" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:48</a> | How can a debater expose false claims without simply calling the other person a liar? | He says an interlocutor has to pick battles and expose the data problem directly, because the culture resists blunt accusations of lying. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=1492" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:52</a> | Was the Falk-Kennedy event really a conversation rather than a debate? | Falk says it was intended as a discussion between scholars, but the topic is emotionally charged because the early-date position functions as a literalism test for many supporters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=1762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:22</a> | Could Falk explain the low, middle, and high chronology discussion further? | He says computer-assisted chronology has changed the field, the high chronology no longer works, Assyrian middle chronology was a compromise, and Egyptian chronology is now closer to a middle-low or low chronology depending on the dynasty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=2307" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:27</a> | Have early-date proponents stopped saying Avaris was abandoned under Amenhotep II and shifted to saying only the palatial district was abandoned? | Falk says Kennedy lowered the bar by speaking of upheaval rather than abandonment, because Bietak does not show that the whole city of Avaris was abandoned under Amenhotep II. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=2637" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:57</a> | Does Rabbi Hillel Hanasi's chronology stand as an argument if its Jerusalem destruction date is wrong? | Falk has little confidence in arguments based on Seder Olam alone; he thinks Lepsius may have reached the right ballpark, but ancient sources are poor guides to events long before their own time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=3004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:04</a> | How do relative chronological synchronisms get tied to absolute calendar dates? | He points to agreed anchor points such as the death of Josiah and Nekau II's invasion, says later dates are solidly chronicled, and argues that pre-664 BC dates rely on many synchronisms that need computer analysis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=3493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:13</a> | Which dates for Ramesses II does Falk prefer, and why did Kitchen's dates shift? | He prefers 1288-1222 BC and says Kitchen moved to 1279 because of an extended 28-year reign for Horemheb, which Falk rejects because Horemheb's wine dockets support a shorter reign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=3646" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:46</a> | Should Septuagint variants in Kings be taken into account for biblical chronology? | Falk generally leans toward the Masoretic text for the reigns of the kings, while acknowledging variants and depending on Thiele's later amended chronology with Kitchen's comments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=3923" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:23</a> | Could Shishak's attack have occurred in Rehoboam's first sole year after Solomon's death? | Falk thinks the proposal may create consistency problems with later co-regencies and successors, so it would need to be tested in the chronology calculations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=4132" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:52</a> | If Solomon had the land, plans, and contracts, why did he start building the temple only in his fourth year? | Falk says the text gives the fourth year, but the reason is unknown; a need to stabilize the kingdom or a short David-Solomon co-regency is possible but speculative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=4261" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:01</a> | What does Falk think of Ralph Hawkins's argument that the Exodus term may refer to squads rather than thousands? | He respects Hawkins and has no particular problem with the view, but says the passage could involve numerical exaggeration or mistranslation and cannot be pinned down with certainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=4371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:51</a> | How would Falk respond to the claim that Exodus co-opts Ramesside propaganda rather than recording history? | He says the objection assumes borrowing, whereas Joshua Berman's argument is about shared late Bronze Age literary forms and context, not direct literary dependence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=4701" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:21</a> | Did David Rohl's chronology affect Kennedy's position, and what about ABR in general? | Falk does not think Rohl directly shaped Kennedy's position; he sees Kennedy as much more aligned with ABR's strict early-date and high-chronology approach. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=4861" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:01</a> | How should the 450 years in Acts 13:20 be understood? | Falk says Acts 13 is textually and interpretively difficult, the starting point for the 450 years is debated, and it should not be used as a crux for an early-date argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=5415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:15</a> | If Kennedy accepted the chronological alignment correction, would the archaeology match Hoffmeier's late-date view? | Falk says Kennedy did not contest the late Bronze destruction layer itself; the problem is trying to attach that destruction to Deborah rather than reading the strata reports correctly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=5668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:28</a> | What does Falk think about reading Hebrew *suf* as a sea rather than reeds? | He says that confuses different periods of Hebrew; in the Pentateuch *suf* means reeds, and the sea reading appears later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=6040" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:40</a> | Why not take the 480 years in 1 Kings 6:1 symbolically or approximately? | Falk says Kennedy's "exact almost" language is incoherent, and the early-date argument generally treats the number as exact even though the broader numerology and variants complicate that claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=6347" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:47</a> | How does Falk answer criticism that he seemed too harsh in the debate? | He says he intended to focus on arguments and be diplomatic, but shifted when he judged that incompetence and bad teaching needed to be exposed for viewers' benefit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=6559" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:19</a> | Could Judges 11 involve Ammonites working with Moabites against Israel? | Falk says cooperation is possible in theory, but the text gives no direct evidence; it presents a dispute between Jephthah and the Ammonites over land taken from the Amorites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=6986" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:26</a> | How does the Judges 11 Ammonite-Amorite issue connect to the early-date versus late-date debate? | He explains that early daters add Jephthah's 300 years to an approximate date for Jephthah to reach around 1400 BC, making Judges 11 one of their standard proof texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=7277" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:17</a> | Does Hoffmeier make a similar plausibility argument for the Exodus? | Falk distinguishes Hoffmeier and Kitchen's inductive probability arguments from Kennedy's plausibility arguments, saying the former compile data with explanatory force while the latter lower the evidential bar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=7583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:23</a> | Are historical theories rarely strictly falsifiable because they can always be adjusted? | Falk says historical arguments can still be tested by challenging their premises, while an argument that is structured so it cannot be falsified has a serious evidential problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=7756" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:16</a> | Should any Exodus-date view require evidence of upheaval during the relevant pharaoh's reign? | Falk says no, because the text does not require broad national upheaval; some plagues target specific agricultural cycles, and the firstborn deaths are hard to estimate archaeologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=8037" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:57</a> | Should the 600 chariots in Exodus be taken literally, and how many Egyptian chariots may have been lost? | He thinks Pharaoh gathered the elite chariot core plus nearby garrisons, estimating roughly three thousand chariots could have been involved, not infantry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=8229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:09</a> | Where were Egypt's main chariot garrisons, and were there chariots in the south toward Kush? | Falk names Per-Ramesses, Tjaru, Migdol, and Elim as key locations, and says the south was mainly infantry territory because the terrain was poor for chariots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=8447" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:47</a> | Has Falk lost friends over the late-date view, and is the debate as toxic as Genesis origins debates? | He says yes, he has lost friends and faced seminary consequences, and he considers the Exodus-date debate even more toxic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=9028" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:28</a> | Why do some scholars seem willing to hint at both early-date and late-date positions rather than defend one openly? | He said the date of the Exodus is a publishing and constituency flashpoint, so some scholars hedge to avoid alienating readers or institutions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=9213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:33</a> | Is Kenneth Kitchen like the egyptologist version of Ben Shapiro? | Falk says Kitchen is blunt and will directly say when he is unconvinced, then back it up with detailed critique. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=9617" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:17</a> | Was Falk at Liverpool for three years, and how did he move from philosophy and IT into Egyptology? | He says he worked in IT for nearly 20 years, used an employer education benefit to begin seminary, then moved through multiple graduate degrees into PhD work and research with Kenneth Kitchen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=10713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:33</a> | Where does Falk get the view that Genesis genealogical ages refer to clans or peoples? | He points to clan-based structures in the Old Testament, honorific transposition, abbreviated genealogies, and the broad use of clan categories in Israelite society. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=11060" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:20</a> | How would Falk answer the claim that the Genesis ages systematically decrease after the flood? | He says later groups and notable figures can rise faster as societies accelerate and as the historical record becomes more expansive closer to the present. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=11400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:00</a> | Was the Falk-Kennedy debate too short? | Falk says it was too short; McDowell had a hard stop and the debate only reached about half of Kennedy's planned agenda. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=11851" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:31</a> | Why does the absence of radiocarbon dates from ABR's el-Maqatir presentations raise a red flag? | Falk says Levantine radiocarbon dates tend to run older and work better for relative than absolute dating, and ABR would not benefit from dates that come out older than desired. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=12057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:57</a> | What should someone do if they accidentally bought Titus Kennedy's *Unearthing the Bible*? | Falk says the pictures are good, but he considers the commentary shallow and substandard. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=12188" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:08</a> | What are Kennedy's credentials, and is he fit to speak on the topic? | Falk says Kennedy is bona fide and credentialed, with a PhD from the University of South Africa, but he thinks Kennedy was not well trained. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=12526" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:46</a> | Can Falk give an example of how Sean McDowell's edited version made Kennedy look worse? | He says some freeze-and-recovery context was removed, leaving Kennedy appearing to stare silently before moving to the next subject. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=13303" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:43</a> | Is a Sinai Peninsula location for Mount Sinai a problem because it lies near Egyptian sites like Serabit el-Khadim and Wadi Feiran? | Falk says Serabit el-Khadim was seasonally occupied, mining-focused, and separated from likely Israelite locations, so it does not create the danger assumed by the question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=13829" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:29</a> | If Egyptians and Israelites had interacted militarily near Sinai, would the Israelites have recorded it? | Falk says yes, and therefore the burden of proof is on anyone claiming such a conflict occurred. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=14022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:42</a> | Is a second Falk-Kennedy debate likely? | Falk doubts it will happen, but says he would be willing and was not intimidated by Kennedy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=14863" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:43</a> | Is Falk a biblical scholar, or only an egyptologist? | He says his PhD is in Egyptology, but his master's work and specialization in Exodus and the Pentateuch give him sufficient biblical-scholarship expertise in that area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=15216" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:13:36</a> | Was Falk still mad about the Deuteronomy 32 question that led to the Kip Davis controversy? | He says he was not personally upset with the questioner, and the later criticism grew from opponents watching his Q&A after prior critiques of Josh Bowen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=17953" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:59:13</a> | Is James Hoffmeier working on a new edition of *Israel in Egypt*? | Falk says Hoffmeier told him he was working on it, but that such plans should be taken with caution until they actually appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=18052" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:00:52</a> | Is Hoffmeier's essay in *Five Views on the Exodus* his latest defense of the Exodus? | Falk agrees it is Hoffmeier's latest engagement, but wishes the volume had used younger scholars more closely engaged with current argumentation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=18180" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:03:00</a> | Would Ronald Hendel be the strongest representative of his view in *Five Views on the Exodus*? | Falk thinks Hendel is probably the most erudite representative of that view and is not easy to replace with a younger scholar. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=18554" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:09:14</a> | How should Christians answer the Muslim claim that Exodus 5 describes a pilgrimage to Mecca? | Falk says the text is about a feast in the wilderness, not a Hajj, and the travel time to Mecca does not fit the three-day framework. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=18780" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:13:00</a> | What does Falk think of Barry Beitzel being used by *Patterns of Evidence*? | He says Beitzel is a brilliant geographer and a friend, but he dislikes seeing a long-retired scholar used in that production context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3frfGREytOQ?t=18950" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:15:50</a> | Was there a wood shortage in Egypt during the late date, as IP mentions in his updated Exodus video? | Falk says yes; evidence appears from the end of Dynasty 20 into Dynasty 21, including coffin reuse and recycling during Dynasty 21. |
